scope.title: Synthetic monthly retail reporting semantics
scope.purpose: Define reviewed metric, dimension, period, and analysis-validity semantics for the packaged Reporting Engine fixture.
included_subjects: brand, category, region, and SKU analysis

supports: mechanical metric, dimension, and period candidates
caveats: The profile does not establish business meaning or analysis validity.
The source describes a synthetic test fixture, not a real business.

evidence.claim: The referenced physical fields and mechanical role candidates exist in the profiled dataset.
notes: Mechanical evidence does not approve a business interpretation.

evidence.claim: Each row is one SKU-brand observation for one calendar month; current YTD and aligned prior-year YTD are valid reusable period rules.
Sales is additive net invoiced sales in US dollars.
Units is additive sold unit volume.
MarginRate is non-additive and must be aggregated as a sales-weighted mean.
Average selling price is sum(Sales) divided by sum(Units) for the selected scope.
Brand, Category, Region, and SKU have the documented reporting and entity roles.
notes: Brand and SKU are redundant as simultaneous decomposition dimensions in this fixture.

evidence.claim: The listed time, ranking, mix, relationship, distribution, and PVM analyses are supported; funnel, statement, cohort, set, and root-cause analyses are not established.

metrics.label: Sales
metrics.definition: Net invoiced sales value at the monthly SKU-brand row grain.
metrics.rationale: The canonical fixture notes define Sales as additive USD value.
caveats: An increase is descriptive; the fixture does not assert that it is always favorable.

metrics.label: Units
metrics.definition: Sold unit volume at the monthly SKU-brand row grain.
metrics.rationale: The canonical fixture notes define Units as additive sold volume.

metrics.label: Sales_per_Units
metrics.definition: Average selling price for the selected scope, calculated as the ratio of aggregate sales to aggregate units.
metrics.rationale: The source notes define average selling price as a ratio of sums, correcting the profiler's generic derived-rate candidate into a reviewed metric.
caveats: Requires non-zero aggregate units in the selected scope.

inference_reasons: additive value metric divided by additive volume metric

metrics.label: MarginRate
metrics.definition: Gross margin divided by sales at the monthly SKU-brand row grain.
metrics.rationale: The canonical source defines MarginRate and its sales-weighted aggregation rule.
caveats: Do not sum rates or use an unweighted mean across unequal sales values.

inference_reasons: name suggests price, rate, or ratio

dimensions.label: Brand
dimensions.definition: Commercial brand used for reporting and aggregation.
dimensions.rationale: The source notes define Brand as a reporting dimension grouped by Category.
caveats: Brand and SKU are one-to-one in this fixture and must not be treated as independent decomposition axes.

dimensions.label: Category
dimensions.definition: Product category grouping brands into Color and Care.
dimensions.rationale: The canonical source defines Category as a brand grouping.

dimensions.label: Region
dimensions.definition: Reporting geography for North and South.
dimensions.rationale: The canonical source defines Region as a reporting geography that cross-classifies Category.

dimensions.label: SKU
dimensions.definition: Stock-keeping-unit identifier for the observation entity.
dimensions.rationale: The canonical source defines SKU as the row entity under Brand.
caveats: One SKU per brand makes SKU redundant with Brand in this fixture.

periods.label: Calendar month
periods.definition: The calendar month represented by the row; stored as the first day of that month.
periods.rationale: The source notes define Date as a calendar-month key rather than a transaction timestamp.
caveats: Display month labels, not the literal first-day storage convention.

period_rules.label: All available calendar months
period_rules.rationale: Ordered monthly trajectory and composition analyses may use the complete available snapshot range.
caveats: The concrete start and end are resolved from each snapshot.

period_rules.label: Current calendar year to latest available month
period_rules.rationale: Current-period ranking, relationship, distribution, and composition analyses use calendar YTD through the latest available month.
caveats: The snapshot supplies the current year and latest available month.

period_rules.label: Current calendar YTD versus aligned prior-year YTD
period_rules.rationale: Current and prior periods must cover aligned calendar months and are resolved from the latest available snapshot month.
caveats: The comparison is unavailable when the snapshot lacks aligned prior-year history.

analysis_policies.label: Monthly sales trajectory
analysis_policies.question_family: How did total sales move across the available calendar months?
analysis_policies.business_purpose: Inspect the ordered monthly path of total sales.
conditions: Aggregate Sales by calendar month before rendering.
analysis_policies.rationale: Sales is additive and Date is a reviewed ordered monthly axis.
caveats: Only four months are present, so seasonality cannot be inferred.

analysis_policies.label: Current versus prior sales by month
analysis_policies.question_family: Where were the monthly sales gaps between the reviewed current and prior-year windows?
analysis_policies.business_purpose: Compare January and February sales with the same months in the prior year.
conditions: Align months by calendar month number within the two reviewed windows.
analysis_policies.rationale: The metric is additive and the source defines equal current and prior-year month windows.

analysis_policies.label: Current brand sales ranking
analysis_policies.question_family: Which brands generated the most sales in the reviewed current period?
analysis_policies.business_purpose: Rank brands by current calendar-YTD sales through the snapshot's latest month.
conditions: Sum Sales by Brand within the current scope.
analysis_policies.rationale: Brand is a reviewed grouping dimension and Sales is additive.

analysis_policies.label: Category sales mix over time
analysis_policies.question_family: How did total sales and category composition change across months?
analysis_policies.business_purpose: Show monthly total sales together with Color and Care contribution.
conditions: Sum Sales by calendar month and Category.
analysis_policies.rationale: Sales is additive and Category is a reviewed composition dimension.

analysis_policies.label: Brand sales and margin relationship
analysis_policies.question_family: Which current-period brands combine higher sales with higher or lower margin rate?
analysis_policies.business_purpose: Compare current-period brand scale with sales-weighted margin rate.
conditions: Sum Sales by Brand and calculate MarginRate as a Sales-weighted mean.
analysis_policies.rationale: The source explicitly supports a Brand-level sales-versus-margin relationship after reviewed aggregation.
caveats: The four-point fixture is illustrative and cannot establish statistical association.

analysis_policies.label: Brand sales, margin, and units bubble view
analysis_policies.question_family: How do current brands compare on sales, margin rate, and unit scale?
analysis_policies.business_purpose: Add current unit scale to the Brand sales-versus-margin relationship.
conditions: Aggregate Sales and Units by sum and MarginRate by Sales-weighted mean.
analysis_policies.rationale: Sales, Units, MarginRate, and Brand have compatible reviewed semantics for a scoped entity-level relationship.
caveats: Bubble area is descriptive magnitude, not a fourth causal claim.

analysis_policies.label: Margin-rate distribution
analysis_policies.question_family: What is the distribution shape of row-level margin rates across the available brand-month observations?
analysis_policies.business_purpose: Inspect spread and concentration of observed row-level margin rates.
conditions: Use the sixteen row-level brand-month observations without aggregating MarginRate first.
analysis_policies.rationale: MarginRate is a numeric observation at the documented row grain and the question explicitly concerns that observation distribution.
caveats: The sample is small; a smoothed density interpretation is not approved.

analysis_policies.label: Sales price-volume-mix decomposition
analysis_policies.question_family: How did the sales change between the reviewed current and prior-year windows decompose into price, volume, and mix effects?
analysis_policies.business_purpose: Decompose the comparable-window sales movement using additive value and volume semantics.
conditions: Use identical Brand-SKU population and equal calendar-month windows.
Derive price as the ratio of aggregate Sales to aggregate Units at the required decomposition grain.
analysis_policies.rationale: The source defines compatible additive value, additive volume, derived price, and comparable period windows.
caveats: The fixture supports a mechanical example; business interpretation of mix effects remains context-dependent.

analysis_policies.label: Category and region sales composition
analysis_policies.question_family: How is current sales distributed jointly across Region and Category?
analysis_policies.business_purpose: Show current sales size and composition across two cross-classifying dimensions.
conditions: Sum Sales by Region and Category within the current scope.
analysis_policies.rationale: Region and Category cross-classify in the fixture and Sales is additive.
caveats: Do not substitute Brand and SKU as the two dimensions because they are redundant in this fixture.

analysis_policies.label: Structured statement reporting
analysis_policies.question_family: Show a structured financial statement with line items and scenarios.
analysis_policies.business_purpose: Record that the dataset cannot support a statement table despite containing numeric values.
analysis_policies.rationale: The source explicitly states that no statement structure, statement line, or scenario semantics are present.
caveats: A different prepared package with reviewed statement roles would require a separate semantic layer or revision.

notes: Review applies only to the synthetic fixture and its canonical source notes.
Contract validation proves wiring, not the truth of semantic judgments in another dataset.

boundaries.deterministic_validation_scope: Schema, identifiers, evidence references, profile bindings, manifest intent references, and required role coverage only.
